a_issues: Lacks a clear release date detail and specifics on Perkins' next Neon project; sources include lower-quality social media posts (Instagram, Facebook) that add little factual value; does not mention Perkins/Ferguson's first-look deal with Neon, which adds useful context to the Paramount/Neon relationship.
b_issues: No major factual flaws; content is consistent with the anchor and well-sourced (Variety, Deadline, Wikipedia, AV Club).
reasoning: Both sets confirm the core facts: Paramount picked up the new Longlegs project (not a direct sequel but set in the Longlegs universe), with Osgood Perkins writing/directing and Nicolas Cage starring, while Neon distributed the original 2024 film. However, Content B provides more reliable and detailed sourcing (Variety, Deadline, Wikipedia, AV Club) with consistent facts, including additional context like Perkins' first-look deal with Neon and his upcoming Neon project 'The Young People,' as well as a Wikipedia summary confirming Paramount takes over distribution rights from Neon. Content A includes redundant low-quality sources (Instagram and Facebook reposts) that don't add unique reliable information, slightly weakening its overall quality. Both are factually accurate and non-contradictory, but B's sourcing depth and added context make it marginally more useful for a complete and accurate answer.
